texto: The truth is, anyone who puts so much of herself and her life into art as you do must naturally fear any failure in that art as a potential threat to your life. And so you protect your art more than you protect your health or the common forms of happiness the rest of us have. And you probably have this in common with every artist you admire.
explicacao: Orientação direta de Arthur Phillips sobre enfrentar incertezas com ação. A afirmação “The truth is, anyone who puts so much of herself and her life into art as you do must naturally f…” funciona como lembrete prático ligado a reflexao, art, artist, útil em momentos de artist e music.

frase_original: The truth is, anyone who puts so much of herself and her life into art as you do must naturally fear any failure in that art as a potential threat to your life. And so you protect your art more than you protect your health or the common forms of happiness the rest of us have. And you probably have this in common with every artist you admire.

texto: He fell in love with Manhattan's skyline, like a first-time brothel guest falling for a seasoned professional. He mused over her reflections in the black East River at dusk, dawn, or darkest night, and each haloed light-in a tower or strung along the jeweled and sprawling spider legs of the Brooklyn Bridge's spans-hinted at some meaning, which could be understood only when made audible by music and encoded in lyrics.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Arthur Phillips: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de vínculos afetivos e cuidado mútuo. O trecho “He fell in love with Manhattan's skyline, like a first-time brothel guest falling for a seasoned …” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que reflexao, new-york-city, manhattan pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de reflexao-noturna e boa-noite.

texto: Maria, groaning for scraps, would drape his head on my feet as I ate, trying to camouflage himself as my napkin or the rug.

texto: So why did poor artists originally hang around in cafes?""I don't know. Inspiration from the atmosphere.""Ha! No, you've been tricked, too, just like the rest of us. Cafes didn't have inspirational atmosphere at first. That only came later, when you knew artists had been hanging around in them.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Arthur Phillips: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de limites e escolhas conscientes. O trecho “So why did poor artists originally hang around in cafes?""I don't know. Inspiration from the atmo…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que motivacao, humor, verdade pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de inspiracao e verdade.

texto: You deicde, and you make our night what you want. Brilliant and ours. Stupid and theirs.
explicacao: Citação de Arthur Phillips sobre temas ligados a reflexao. “You deicde, and you make our night what you want. Brilliant and ours. Stupid and theirs.” articula reflexao, brilliant, night em registro de reflexao, com relevância para contextos como boa-noite e reflexao-noturna.
